title: Oncologist + patient-advocate blocking sign-off + 'not medical advice' / readability verification for patient-facing layer (HIGH tier, deferred out of v1)
context: Any patient-facing content is HIGH risk-tier and cannot ship without blocking sign-off from both a credentialed oncologist and a patient advocate. This is a hard gate — no maintainer can override a 'do not ship' on substance. The oncologist verifies clinical safety, accuracy, and the 'not medical advice' framing; the advocate verifies plain-language faithfulness and that nothing reads as a recommendation. Sign-off is version-scoped. The oncologist holds a veto on clinical safety. Both reviewers must have disclosed and cleared conflicts of interest. No patient-facing release under any circumstances without both sign-offs recorded.
objective: Obtain and record version-scoped blocking sign-off for the patient-facing plain-language layer (015) from both a credentialed oncologist and a patient advocate: the oncologist verifies clinical safety, accuracy, and that no content reads as a treatment or clinical recommendation; the advocate verifies plain-language faithfulness, accessibility, and the 'not medical advice' framing. Both sign-offs are recorded in the reviewers ledger with credentials, COI disclosure, and the signed-off version. The oncologist holds a veto on clinical safety.
acceptanceCriteria: GATE: a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate have been secured, their credentials and consent recorded, and their conflicts of interest disclosed and cleared before sign-off begins; the layer does not ship without both

The oncologist (a credentialed MD with oncology specialisation) reviews every page of the patient-facing layer for clinical safety and accuracy, verifies that no content reads as a treatment recommendation, therapy comparison, dosing guidance, or trial selection for any individual, and confirms the 'not medical advice' framing is adequate

The patient advocate reviews every page for plain-language faithfulness, accessibility (non-specialist comprehension), and that no content reads as a recommendation or directive for a patient to take any action
Both sign-offs are recorded version-scoped in the reviewers ledger: reviewer name, credentials, COI disclosure, reviewed version of the patient-facing layer, sign-off date, and any conditions

The oncologist's veto right is documented: a 'do not ship' from the oncologist on clinical safety grounds is a hard block that no maintainer can override; escalation path (second credentialed reviewer or Elyos governance) is recorded
Name-use for both reviewers is limited to the specific versions they approved; their names may not imply endorsement of the broader project
Patient-facing layer does not go live or become publicly accessible until both sign-offs are committed to the repository
output: A committed reviewers ledger entry recording version-scoped, COI-cleared blocking sign-off from both a credentialed oncologist and a patient advocate for the patient-facing plain-language layer — the HIGH-tier gate that makes the layer eligible for public release.
